Ad Text:
About FYI

FYI is a product-led SaaS company built by accountants, for accountants. Our platform unifies cloud document management, automation and practice management into one intuitive solution, helping firms eliminate manual processes and focus on higher-value work.

Behind our product is The FYI Way - our operating system built on clarity, consistency and excellence. We scale with structured processes, automation and AI, clear expectations and measurable outcomes that keep teams aligned and performing at a world-class standard.

About the role


Reporting to the CEO, this role will lead the Internal Systems team focusing on optimisation of systems, integrations, reporting, workflows and AI-enabled operational processes that support scalable business operations across FYI. You will work closely with leaders across Sales, Client Success, Marketing, Product and Engineering to ensure our systems environment remains connected, efficient, secure and aligned to the evolving needs of our fast-growing SaaS business.
 
Key responsibilities
Shape and evolve the internal systems environment that keeps FYI connected, efficient and scaling.
Turn business needs into practical system improvements using automation and AI-enabled workflows.
Lead and develop the Internal Systems team, building a high-performing culture focused on ownership, accountability, improvement and impact.
Create reporting, dashboards and operational insights that support visibility and confident decision-making across the business.
Strengthen governance across systems, workflows, permissions and processes so our operating model remains secure, consistent and scalable.
Support operational scalability through structured systems management, process optimisation and connected operational workflows.
Maintain oversight of integrations and automation frameworks across platforms, including Zapier and other connected systems.
Collaborate cross-functionally to improve operational efficiency, visibility and scalability across FYI.

About you

You’re a structured and commercially minded operational leader who enjoys solving complex business problems through systems, automation and scalable processes.

You bring a strong ownership mindset and are comfortable balancing strategic thinking with hands-on execution in a fast-moving environment, while creating clarity, structure and operational discipline as priorities evolve.

You’ll also need:

Experience leading internal systems, business systems, automation or operational technology functions in a SaaS or technology environment.
Ability to turn business challenges into smarter workflows using automation and AI-enabled processes to improve how teams operate.
Hands-on experience with automation and integration platforms, including Zapier, with a strong understanding of connected operational systems and scalable workflows.
Clear and confident communication skills, with the ability to influence technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience leading high-performing teams in fast-moving, scale-up environments where adaptability, ownership and continuous improvement matter.
Ability to balance strategic priorities with operational delivery and execution.
Based in Sydney or Adelaide with valid working rights in Australia.
Why work with FYI
Highly competitive remuneration aligned to your experience and impact.
Flexible hybrid working arrangements in Sydney or Adelaide.
Professional development opportunities to accelerate your growth as FYI continues to scale globally.
Paid Parental Leave, celebration leave and additional leave at key service milestones.
Regular team events and company retreats that build connection and alignment.
A supportive, high-performing culture where your ideas are encouraged and recognised.
How to apply

If you are driven by impact and excited to help lead what comes next at FYI, we would love to hear from you.

Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your alignment to this position and why you want to join FYI.

Our promise is a fair and considerate recruitment process. You’ll always hear an outcome from us, and we’ll share feedback where we can.

Company description:



Workplace Type: Hybrid


About Newmont


Newmont is the world's leading gold company and a producer of copper, silver, zinc, lead and molybdenum. The company's world-class portfolio of assets, prospects and talent is anchored in favourable mining jurisdictions in Australia, Papua New Guinea, Africa, Latin America and the Caribbean and North America.

Newmont is the only gold producer listed in the S&P 500 Index and is widely recognized for its principled environmental, social and governance practices. The company is an industry leader in value creation, supported by robust safety standards, superior execution and technical proficiency. Newmont was founded in 1921 and has been publicly traded since 1925.



Job description:

The Position

Newmont is seeking an Operational Intelligence Engineer to provide process control analytical solutions across our global mining operations. Utilising advanced process analysis techniques and technologies visualised through real-time monitoring systems, this strategic role bridges process control engineering, data analytics, and metallurgical expertise to transform our minerals processing data into actionable insights.

Role Overview - Operational Intelligence and Analytics

As our Operational Intelligence Engineer, you will leverage tools such as AVEVA OSIsoft PI (OSIPI), PI Asset Framework, PI Vision, Microsoft Power BI, Grafana, SEEQ, MS Excel, Python, MATLAB, R, Azure and emerging Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications, to provide descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive insights into our sites process control operational data. You will collaborate with central operational support, process control engineering, digital, metallurgical and site operational teams to develop analytical studies, continuous monitoring and/or closed loop process control solutions that have a direct impact on the stabilisation of mineral processing, maximisation of minerals recovery, and reduction of production costs.

Your expertise will help us make smarter decisions faster, maintain process stability, and continuously improve our production process controls. Taking on this role, you'll get the opportunity to shape the future of operational intelligence in the mining industry while working with cutting-edge technologies and a diverse range of teams across our global operations



Profile description:



Here is a snapshot of what your day to day activities will look like:

Design and implement process control analytics solutions that enhance the control systems ability to increase production, reduce cost, and improve overall plant efficiency
Create and maintain real-time monitoring systems for process control systems, critical processes and instrumentation
Create and maintain continuous real-time process control monitoring solutions focusing on Short Interval Control (SIC)
Create and maintain soft-sensor solutions that can be utilised for closed loop process control
Collaborate with centralised and site-based metallurgists, operational support engineers, digital SME's, process control engineers and plant operators to implement closed-loop control strategies focused towards stabilising and optimizing processing units like grinding circuits, flotation circuits, and leaching processes
Provide production insights via ad hoc analysis of historical process control data utilising descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analysis techniques and technologies
Establish and maintain KPI dashboards
Develop and maintain a centralised library of best practices for process analytics applications
Lead training sessions on process control analytics tools and mentor site-based technical resources
Participate in global mining process optimization initiatives and contribute to the community of practice

Your training skills and experience:

Minimum 3 years' experience in process control improvements, with significant operational experience in mineral processing or metallurgy
Industry qualifications in an associated field such as Data Science, Metallurgy, Process Control, IT.
Applied knowledge of AVEVA OSIsoft PI historian and PI Asset Framework systems
Data visualization using commercial-off-the-shelf products (i.e., AVEVA PI Vision, MS PowerBI
Grafana, SEEQ, MS Excel) or programmatic methods (i.e., Python, MATLAB, R)
Demonstrated success implementing analytical solutions within the mineral processing industry
Experience with crushing, grinding, flotation, leaching processes.
Strong understanding of mining-specific process variables, recovery calculations, and metallurgical accounting principles
Advanced computer literacy (word, excel, databases, statistical tools)
Skills in analytical story telling via building easy to read and to the point dashboarding
Applied knowledge of advanced process analytics techniques and technologies
Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts to operational teams
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to identify and engage key stakeholders for collaboration
Customer-oriented, enjoys making connections and building trust with people both in-person and at remote locations
Motivated self-starter, goal-oriented, and strong problem-solving abilities
Active team player who celebrates and promotes team successes


We offer:



Your work environment

This permanent position will be offered on a flexible work arrangement based out of Newmont's Perth office in Subiaco, Western Australia. Applicants living in another Australian capital city location or residing near any Newmont Global Hubs will be considered if the ideal candidate.

The position will require flexibility to travel to Newmont office and/or mine site locations on a regular as required basis, including international travel.

Full and unrestricted eligibility for employment in Australia will be essential.

We understand no candidate will meet every single desired qualification. If your experience looks a little different from what we've identified and you think you can bring value to the role, we'd love to learn more about you!

If you feel that you could make a meaningful contribution in this role, we encourage you to discuss this opportunity with your current people leader and how it aligns with your longer-term career goals.

The Opportunity  

As a Funding Specialist joining Medibank, you will use your proven experience with data and analytics to support how Medibank funds healthcare services. You will help design and improve funding models that deliver better health outcomes for members, while managing costs and supporting sustainable provider partnerships.  

What the person will do: 

Analyse and improve healthcare funding models to support better outcomes, performance, and cost management. 

Use data and insights to evaluate initiatives, predict impacts, and provide clear, practical recommendations. 

Act as the analytics key subject matter expert for healthcare funding models used to purchase hospital-based services. 

Partner with teams across Medibank (including contracting, finance, and data science) to design and deliver funding strategies. 

Support hospital contract negotiations with modelling, insights, and subject matter expertise. 

Research relevant literature and keep abreast of new domestic and international funding model developments to inform next generation improvements and opportunities. 

Engage, consult and build productive working relationships with external stakeholders (hospitals, IHACPA) to seek feedback on proposed models and refine designs to accommodate mutually beneficial outcomes. 

Develop reporting, KPIs, and evaluation frameworks to monitor funding performance and identify risks and opportunities. 

Key skills/strengths: 

We are looking for a senior data and analytics professional who has proven funding experience in a similar role in the healthcare industry.  

You will also have:  

Strong analytical thinking and ability to work with complex data. 

Ability to translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations. 

Collaborative mindset, with confidence working across multiple teams and stakeholders. 

Clear and engaging communication skills (written and verbal). 

Attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem solving. 

Experience in SAS and SQL programmin

Job Title: Contract - Senior Consultant - Business Analyst (AI & Data)
Ad Text:
Our purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology in order to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in.

About the Role

As technology evolves at an unprecedented pace, Datacom is looking for a Technical Business Analyst with a strong AI focus to bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI capabilities and real-world business outcomes. You will play a pivotal role in shaping AI-driven solutions, ensuring intelligent systems are designed, documented, and delivered with precision, clarity, and measurable business value.

What You'll DoRequirements Discovery & AI Solution Design
Understand the client's business processes, pain points, and opportunities where AI and automation can deliver value
Elicit and document requirements for AI/ML-powered solutions, including model inputs/outputs, training data needs, and business rules
Define and document AI use cases, including LLM integrations, predictive analytics, intelligent automation, and conversational AI workflows
Map data flows between AI models, consumers, and providers across integrated systems
Documentation & Artefact Creation
Create and manage JIRA tickets including:
Acceptance criteria
Implementation details
AI model behaviour expectations and edge cases
Consumer/provider mappings
Document all solution artefacts in Confluence, including:
UML sequence diagrams for AI-integrated workflows
Integration design patterns (AI APIs, model endpoints, orchestration layers)
Prompt engineering guidelines and LLM behaviour specifications
Mapping tables, endpoints, and environment configurations
Postman collections for AI API testing
Acceptance criteria for AI model outputs and responses
AI-Focused Facilitation & Collaboration
Facilitate workshops and discovery sessions focused on AI opportunity identification and solution scoping
Lead 3 Amigos sessions (Dev, Tester, and BA) with an emphasis on AI model validation, bias considerations, and responsible AI outcomes
Collaborate with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to translate business requirements into model specifications
Champion responsible AI principles, ensuring ethical considerations, explainability, and compliance are embedded into solution design
Data & Integration Design
Define data model requirements to support AI/ML training pipelines and inference outputs
Document HTTP operations, data types, and payload structures for AI model APIs
Design and maintain integration patterns connecting AI services to enterprise systems

Technical AbilityAPI & Integration Skills
Create Swagger/OpenAPI Specifications for AI service contracts — using Swagger Editor, VS Code, or equivalent
Deep understanding of integration patterns: REST APIs, SOAP, event-driven architectures
API contract design and maintenance for AI model endpoints (e.g., OpenAI, Azure AI, AWS Bedrock, custom ML APIs)
Error handling patterns for AI inference failures, model timeouts, and confidence threshold responses
Experience with Message Queuing technologies such as Kafka and ActiveMQ for AI pipeline event streaming
AI & Machine Learning Awareness
Working knowledge of LLMs (Large Language Models) — including prompt engineering, RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation), and fine-tuning concepts
Ability to document AI model behaviour requirements including confidence scoring, hallucination mitigation strategies, and output validation
Understanding of AI/ML pipeline stages: data ingestion, preprocessing, model training, deployment, and monitoring
Familiarity with AI platforms and services such as:
Azure OpenAI / Azure AI Studio
AWS SageMaker / Bedrock
Google Vertex AI
Hugging Face
Awareness of AI governance frameworks, including model explainability (XAI), fairness, and regulatory compliance (e.g., EU AI Act)
Estimation & Delivery
Provide reliable and traceable estimates for AI change requests and solution delivery, accounting for model iteration cycles and data preparation timelines
Design systems from a functional AI perspective, producing draft Design documents and leading client reviews through to sign-off
Contribute to AI solution roadmaps, identifying phased delivery approaches aligned to business priorities

Responsibilities

As a Technical Business Analyst (AI Focus), your day-to-day activities will include:

Produce drafts of Requirements and Design documents for AI-integrated solutions, lead client reviews, and achieve sign-off
Lead the Analysis and Design stage for AI initiatives, arranging and facilitating all relevant workshops and communication
Work closely with Technical Leads, Architects, and Data Scientists, ensuring they have sufficient understanding of functional requirements to design AI system architecture
Collaborate with Test Analysts to define AI-specific testing strategies, including model validation, A/B testing, and output quality benchmarks
Resolve ambiguities raised by developers and testers in relation to AI model specifications, communicating with clients as required
Monitor and communicate scope changes, AI-related risks, and project issues to Technical Leads and stakeholders proactively
Provide the team with AI industry best practices, emerging trends, and governance advice
Contribute to User Guides and AI usage documentation, supporting end-user adoption of AI-powered solutions
Ensure all design and compliance reviews are completed at required checkpoints, including responsible AI assessments

Tools & Experience
Project & Documentation - Confluence, JIRA
API Design & Testing - Swagger/OpenAPI, Postman, SOAP UI
AI Platforms - Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Hugging Face
Data & Monitoring - Splunk, Power BI
Development Environments - IntelliJ, Eclipse, VS CodeSource ControlGit, Bitbucket
Messaging & Streaming - Kafka, ActiveMQ
AI/ML Tooling - LangChain, Prompt Flow, Jupyter Notebooks (awareness)

Professional Qualities
5+ years of experience as a Technical Business Analyst, with demonstrable exposure to AI, ML, or data-driven solutions
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to critically evaluate AI model outputs and business implications
Exceptional attention to detail, particularly when documenting complex AI workflows and edge cases
Deadline-driven with the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced AI delivery environment
Excellent communication skills — able to translate complex AI concepts for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborative team player with experience working in cross-functional Agile teams
A natural curiosity and passion for emerging AI technologies and their practical business applications

About Datacom

With over 6,200 people across our global offices and an annual revenue exceeding $1.2 billion, Datacom is one of Australasia's largest professional IT services companies. We have deep expertise in data centres, IT services, software engineering, application management, and AI-enabled solutions.

As AI reshapes industries, Datacom is at the forefront — partnering with clients to design and deliver intelligent, responsible, and impactful AI solutions. We are committed to hiring, developing, and promoting the best talent from diverse backgrounds.

We are local at heart, yet world-class in capability.

Our purpose

Here at Datacom we connect people and technology in order to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in.

Our Team

The Software Engineering team look after all the digital technology solutions that are currently being used by our clients. We take pride in what we do and who we do it for. As a well-established team we have a good mix of senior through to junior- level roles, and we encourage everyone to come to work in their own skin, be yourself and most importantly have fun.

About the Role

We’re looking for strong intermediate and senior level Business Analysts to join our team and own outcomes with a dedicated customer. You’ll utilize your SDLC expertise along with your general all-round awesome BA skills. This role will involve analytical business analysis, requirements gathering and influencing decisions within the Software Engineering and digital space. You will likely be responsible for leading multiple platform and application development teams, coordinating and scheduling team meetings, measuring progress and monitoring team performance along with your Delivery Manager. You will coordinate with subject matter experts to resolve impediments and understand key inter-team dependencies.

What you’ll do:

Establish and drive requirements gathering activities
Organise and facilitate requirements workshops
Create user personas and user journeys
Creating documentation i.e. use cases, user stories, prototypes, wireframes, proposals and strategy documents
Collaborate with Product Management and Product Ownership team to ensure value is being created and delivery backlogs are groomed and maintained.
Influencing change that increases team performance at a sustainable pace.
Building and promoting an open, trusting and respectful team environment; facilitating discussion, collaborative decision making and conflict resolution
Providing support to the team(s) using a servant leadership style whenever possible and leading by example.
Organizing and facilitating daily stand-ups, requirement estimation, sprint & release planning, demos and other related team meetings.

About you:

Pivotal for success in this team is your commitment to agile principles and practices, along with a passion for driving the customer experience and requirements on technically-lead projects. And of course, a key aspect being your ability to communicate with stakeholders to elicit requirements and seek opportunities to improve their business.

Your background:

Business analyst with experience delivering enterprise projects
Ability to influence stakeholder relationships and drive team performance
Development background and/or knowledge of the SDLC
Excellent documentation and communication skills
An experienced Agile and Scrum practitioner
Scrum Master experience preferred  

About Datacom

We have over 6,200 people across our global offices, and we have extensive expertise in operating data centres, providing IT services, software engineering and application management, as well as payroll and customer service design and operations. With this comes a long list of significant clients Datacom is committed to hiring, developing and promoting the best talent from a diverse range of backgrounds. We are local at heart, yet world-class in capability.

Hi there! We’re Five Good Friends. 

We’re an approved aged care and NDIS service provider helping our Members (others call them clients) live vibrantly in their own homes. We wrap a care team around them and their families, supported by innovative software that coordinates in the background—so our teams can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care. 

We’re proud to be a place where purpose meets impact. Every day, our work contributes to helping people live their best lives, and that starts with our people. 

As more people choose Five Good Friends, we’re building out our Platform team to strengthen the foundations that power our technology and services. That’s where you come in. 

About the Role 

We’re looking for an AI System Analyst to help us understand, define, and shape how our platform and operations work end-to-end. 

This role sits at the intersection of business, systems, and engineering. You’ll work across processes, SaaS platforms, integrations, and data turning real-world operational complexity into clear, structured knowledge that supports both delivery teams and AI-assisted ways of working. 

You won’t be working in isolation or producing static documentation. Instead, you’ll be embedded in the work, partnering closely with engineers, product, and operational teams to uncover how things work, identify gaps, and bring clarity to ambiguity. 

You'll be part of a hands-on, genuinely collaborative team of engineers, system thinkers and innovators. Think getting around a whiteboard together, unpacking problems, challenging ideas, and designing solutions as a group. The work is substantive; the problems are challenging; and expectations on the whole Platform team to continue delivering are high. 

This is a hybrid role, balancing in-office collaboration with focused work from home. When you’re in, you’ll be based in our modern South Bank office, close to great cafes and restaurants, with easy access to public transport. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Investigating and documenting business processes, workflows, and system behaviours 
Tracing how workflows across SaaS platforms, integrations, APIs, and automations 
Defining business rules, operational terminology, and data definitions 
Creating structured, usable knowledge that supports engineering delivery and AI-enabled workflows 
Identifying gaps, inconsistencies, and dependencies across systems and processes 
Collaborating with engineers, product, and stakeholders to ensure shared understanding before delivery 
Supporting more consistent, scalable ways of working across the platform 

About You 

You’re naturally curious and think in systems, you want to understand how things actually work, not just how they’re supposed to. You’re comfortable navigating complexity, asking the right questions, and bringing structure to ambiguity. 

You enjoy working closely with both technical and non-technical people, and you’re confident moving between operational conversations and technical detail. 

You’ll bring: 

Strong systems thinking and analytical capability 
Experience working across complex operational or SaaS-heavy environments 
The ability to translate real-world processes into clear, structured documentation 
Strong written and verbal communication skills 
Experience working in agile or iterative delivery environments 
A proactive, detail-oriented approach with a focus on quality and clarity 

You’re also energised by AI and actively using AI-assisted tools or working with AI/ML concepts, and excited by how data platforms enable intelligent systems. 

Bonus points for experience with APIs, integrations, automation tools, data or process mapping, or working alongside engineering teams. 

What’s in It for You 

You’ll help shape how our platform and the knowledge behind it supports better care and stronger outcomes for our members. Your work will enable faster, clearer, and more confident delivery across the organisation, while contributing to how we embed AI into the way we work. 

And a few more things we think you’ll value: 

Competitive salary package that recognises your skills and experience 
Flexible hybrid work arrangements to support focus and collaboration 
Annual $500 health and wellbeing allowance 
Additional annual leave accrual after your second year and the option to purchase extra leave 
Free confidential counselling through Sonder 
Parental leave top-ups for eligible caregivers, including superannuation support 
Extend our care to a loved one at a special rate 
A workplace with an employee engagement score 17% above industry average 
And after 5 years of continuous service—a trip to Okinawa, Japan, the community that inspired Five Good Friends 

How to Apply 

If this role sounds like you, click the apply button and follow the prompts. Please include your CV and a cover letter telling us a bit about yourself and why this role is a great fit. 

Please, no external recruiters or agencies.

About the Company

Our client is a growing Australian business operating across manufacturing, distribution and direct-to-consumer channels. With a well-established customer base and national operations, the company is currently investing heavily in its systems, reporting capability, and commercial infrastructure to support continued growth.

The business is undergoing a significant transformation phase, with a strong focus on improving analytics, decision support, operational visibility, and scalability across the organisation. This role will play a key part in supporting that journey while partnering closely with senior leadership across finance and operations.

The Role

This is a newly created Commercial Manager position sitting at the intersection of commercial finance, analytics, and strategic support. Reporting directly to the CFO, you will play a key role in building out the commercial reporting and analytical capability of the business while partnering closely with senior leadership on strategic initiatives.

A core focus of the role will be owning and continuously evolving the Power BI environment, improving dashboards, data models, reporting logic and commercial insights across manufacturing, retail and e-commerce operations.

This role offers genuine breadth, visibility, and long-term career opportunity within a fast-growing PE-backed environment.
 

What You’ll Do
Own the Power BI reporting and analytics environment across the business
Build dashboards across sales, operations, e-commerce and financial performance
Develop and maintain data models and ERP/system integrations
Lead budgeting, forecasting and commercial reporting processes
Build financial models to support strategic initiatives and business growth
Deliver meaningful commercial insights and KPI reporting to leadership
Partner with manufacturing, operations and sales teams on performance analysis
Support pricing, margin analysis and product profitability initiatives
Assist with board reporting and investor analysis
Support the CFO on strategic projects, commercial modelling and growth initiatives
What We’re Looking For
CA or CPA qualified
Strong background in commercial finance, FP&A or management accounting
Advanced hands-on Power BI capability
Strong financial modelling and analytical skills
Experience within manufacturing, retail, consumer goods or e-commerce environments highly regarded
Commercially minded with strong stakeholder engagement skills
Why You’ll Love It
Newly created role with genuine scope and influence
Direct exposure to an experienced commercial CFO
PE-backed growth business with exciting expansion plans
Broad exposure across manufacturing, retail and e-commerce
High visibility role with senior leadership exposure
Opportunity to build and shape reporting capability from the ground up
To Apply

Please apply through the relevant link, or to discuss this opportunity in more detail, please contact Tom Clarke at TCl••••@sharpandcarter.com.au.

Company description:



ATTURRA OPERATIONS PTY LTD



Job description:



What you'll be doing

At Atturra, as a Senior Integration Consultant, you'll be working on some of the highest profile transformation and technology projects in the Asia Pacific region.

The depth, breadth and scope of our projects means you are continually developing your skillset and the value you can deliver.

These are projects that are making a real difference in people's lives, allowing you to build the future, together.

Key Responsibilities

Contribute to technical design discussions.
Lead or contribute to code reviews and technical design/specifications.
Lead or contribute to development of custom code.
Contribute to the enhancement of in-house delivery methodologies.
Contribute to the review and analysis of business requirements.
Perform technical assessments and sizing.
Contribute to the delivery process by being part of client meetings, making presentations, and at times working as part of the tender response team.

Working Conditions

Full time permanent opportunity
Based in Melbourne

What will make you succesful?

The position requires a highly motivated individual who can provide strong technical skills, along with leadership, guidance, and coaching abilities, to deliver quality focused outcomes.

At least 10 years of experience in a similar role or capacity.
Experience linking cross-functional applications with Boomi Integration Platform experience.
Knowledge of API Management and API Gateway Technology (such as Apigee, Axway, Azure, AWS, etc.)
Experience in integrating with core enterprise software applications (such as Salesforce, Oracle, SAP, NetSuite, MS Dynamics 365, Workday, Sage etc.), both on-premises and cloud.
Experience with business and technical requirement analysis, business process modelling/mapping, methodology development, and data mapping.
Strong understanding of enterprise application integration patterns.
Expertise in the assessment, architectural design, and implementation of integration patterns including middleware, bulk data integration, real-time data integrations, file-based integrations, cloud integrations, process integrations, and event processing.
Expertise with B2B integration concepts, standards, architectures (AS2, X12, EDIFACT, XML).
Knowledge of relational database design and data management techniques.

You will also have:

Excellent communication skills & the ability to consult with stakeholders at all levels.
Demonstrated ability to contribute and self-manage within a small team environment.
Ability to build rapport within the workplace, work well within a team, excellent networking skills and the ability to perform in a fast-paced environment.
Business acumen and ability to identify client business drivers.

Why is this an exciting opportunity?

Atturra is one of the fastest growing ASX-listed advisory and IT solutions companies in Australia, giving you a strong foundation to fulfil your career ambitions.

We currently have 1100+ professionals working across Australia, New Zealand, Singapore and Hong Kong who are using innovation to lead the way.

The Data & Integration business unit helps clients unlock the full potential of data by integrating systems and applications, enabling digital transformation through our comprehensive on-premises, hybrid, and cloud integration solutions. We specialise in Boomi, Smartsheet, and OpenText.

At Atturra we embrace innovation within our teams, fostering an environment of possibility and growth. We believe in creating a supportive and welcoming culture, where everyone is encouraged to achieve success through collaboration, hard work and dedication.

A strong set of company values

Our culture is reflected by our employees who demonstrate camaraderie, innovation, dedication, respect and excellence in everything they do. These traits have become integral to the way we do business and are the values we strive for.

Career growth via succession planning, internal mobility programs, and mentorship opportunities.

Employee benefits, recognition, and wellbeing platform.Mental health support through our Employee Assistance Program.

About QIC

As one of Australias leading institutional investment managers with more than $100 billion in assets under management, we have a presence across four continents, seven offices and assets across global markets.

At QIC, we believe in shared value - where delivering strong investment returns can go hand in hand with doing good for our clients, people and communities. If we invest well, we will all do better together.

Life at QIC

We know that our people perform at their best when they feel a sense of belonging and are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day.  We provide a supportive work environment where everyone can thrive and feel valued for their meaningful contribution in delivering for the prosperity of our clients, people and communities.

Joining QIC, you can contribute to our employee led networks that champion our diversity, equity and inclusion strategy, which consists of five key pillars: Gender Equality, LGBTQ+, Accessibility, Cultural & Linguistic Diversity, and First Nations People.

Other reasons to join us:

Flexibility and hybrid options encouraged – certified Family Inclusive Workplace
Recharge leave - additional 3 days leave per year
Annual Fitness/home office reimbursement
Ability to swap public holidays for those of personal, cultural or religious significance
26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leave
Financial study assistance and study leave
Community leave for supporting the causes you love
Development opportunities to extend, expand and enhance your growth.

About the role

QIC’s Private Debt team is offering a 6-month fixed-term opportunity for an experienced Business Analyst. Supporting operational excellence across our Infrastructure Debt and Private Debt Australia platforms, this role reports to the General Manager, Operations Private Debt. It plays a key role in strengthening processes, data integrity, reporting, governance, and client service as the team continues to grow and evolve.

This is a hands‑on, detail‑driven role where you’ll collaborate with investment teams across Brisbane, Sydney, London and New York, as well as key corporate partners. Your work will directly support data requirements aligned to operational and client outcomes, including investment execution, fund operations, client servicing needs and ESG reporting, all aligned to QIC’s purpose of creating shared value.

Key responsibilities 

Manage and analyse portfolio cashflow and performance data, including cash yield calculations, as well as maintaining fund cash models.
Maintain the Private Debt information library, support quarterly valuations, contribute to investor reporting and due‑diligence responses and update or prepare charts and PowerPoint materials as required
Coordinate periodic fund reporting and monitor correspondence, invoicing and various obligations connected with third‑party administrators for Luxembourg‑domiciled funds.
Support business responsibilities including ESG reporting (e.g. AASB2), QIC policy adherence and maintaining risk registers.
Identify opportunities to enhance efficiency, accuracy and controls across operational workflows, reporting and internal systems.
Work closely with the investment team, and QIC corporate stakeholders across Client Solutions & Capital, Finance, Risk, ESG and Investment Operations to ensure seamless communication and delivery.
Contribute to the development and implementation of new and evolving team initiatives.

 

About you

This role is ideal for someone with 3–5 years’ experience in financial services, finance or investment operations, private markets or a related environment who is ready to step into a dynamic, growth‑focused team.

You’re a proactive, detail‑oriented team player with a genuine interest in financial markets, private debt as an asset class and investment operations. You thrive in fast‑paced environments, enjoy solving problems and communicate with clarity and confidence. You build strong relationships and take pride in delivering high‑quality work that supports both clients and colleagues.

Skills

Strong understanding of financial and performance data, with the ability to interpret and present insights.
Comfortable navigating and maintaining Excel / financial models.
Ability to translate data into clear, compelling charts and slides for client audiences.
Experience navigating internal systems and supporting reporting processes.
Strong written and verbal communication, with the ability to tailor information to stakeholders.
Ability to manage competing deadlines with accuracy and composure.
A collaborative, supportive approach with a willingness to learn and contribute.

 

Why this Role?
An opportunity to make an immediate impact in a 6‑month contract, supporting operational excellence across a growing Private Debt platform within a collaborative, high-performing team.

Application
Apply with your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience

QIC is sourcing directly for this role and is not engaging recruitment agencies. We do not accept unsolicited resumes, and QIC is not responsible for any fees associated with any unsolicited submissions.

If you’re a data scientist with strong ML and deep learning foundations who’s ready to stop building proofs of concept and start putting them into production, this role is for you.

We’re partnering with a well-established, global organisation to find a Senior Data Scientist to join a team an existing and capable team that has already done the hard work of proving what’s possible. The proof of concept is done. Reporting directly to the Head of Machine Learning, this role is about taking those solutions and making them real — productionising models, building robust AI pipelines, and delivering outcomes.
 
 
What you’ll be doing
 

Productionise AI and ML solutions that have already been validated at proof-of-concept stage — your job is to make them stable, scalable, and real.
Build and maintain AI pipelines and deployment infrastructure using MLOps best practices on Azure.
Develop, deploy, and maintain advanced models across NLP/LLMs, time-series forecasting, computer vision, and geospatial analytics.
Work with LLM development and redeployment, extending your classical ML foundations into modern generative AI approaches.
Partner with stakeholders and cross-functional teams to communicate progress clearly and ensure seamless integration of AI solutions.
Collaborate with infrastructure teams to ensure solutions are reliable and production-grade from day one.
Contribute to a small, high-quality team where your work has direct visibility and impact.
 
 
What we’re looking for
 

4+ years of professional experience developing, deploying, and maintaining data science and ML models in production environments.
Strong foundations in machine learning and deep learning
Proven experience building classification, regression, or forecasting models and taking them to production.
Experience working with LLMs — development, fine-tuning, and redeployment.
Hands-on experience building AI pipelines and deploying models in managed cloud environments, ideally Azure.
MLOps experience — you know what it takes to keep a model healthy in production.
Solid grounding in the Python scientific stack: NumPy, SciPy, scikit-learn, pandas.
Experience across NLP techniques including NER, semantic analysis, text classification, and topic modelling.
Familiarity with time-series forecasting, computer vision, and geospatial technologies is advantageous.
 
 
The team & culture
You’ll be joining a team of data scientists and ML practitioners working on genuinely complex problems. The team is led by a Head of ML and AI who has built a function that takes its craft seriously. AI sits on the executive agenda — the reporting line goes directly to the CIO — so the work you do here has real organisational weight behind it.
 
 
Why this role?

The POC is done — you’re here to build, not explore. High impact from day one.
Genuine technical depth in a small team where everyone pulls their weight.
Strong leadership — a manager who has done this before and knows what good looks like.
AI is a board-level priority, not a side project.
Hybrid working ( requirement for 3 days in the office) with onsite parking.
Competitive salary package commensurate with your experience and the seniority of the role.
 
 
Apply via the link below, or reach out directly to me at mar••@bluefinresources.com.au

Senior Manager | Technology Finance / Strategic Finance

Senior visibility and exposure across large-scale Technology portfolios
Broad mix of FP&A, business partnering, investment and decision support work
High-impact role within a leading Financial Services organisation

Discovery

Emesent is a world leader in autonomous drone technology, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Our solutions are transforming how mining, engineering, infrastructure, and public safety industries capture, understand, and act on spatial data. We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own and drive the next generation of our products.


As a Senior Product Manager, you will own the strategy and execution for one or more product lines within Emesent’s portfolio. You’ll work at the intersection of hardware, software, and autonomy defining what we build, why, and how we bring it to market. This is a high-autonomy role where you’ll set direction, make trade-offs, and drive outcomes. You’ll collaborate closely with engineering leads, commercial teams, and users to ensure our products deliver measurable value in complex, real-world environments.

What you’ll do

Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for your area. Translate business objectives, market signals, and user needs into a clear, prioritised plan that balances near-term delivery with longer-term bets

Customer & Market Insight: Deeply understand our users workflows, pain points, and environments. Conduct field visits, user research, and competitive analysis to ensure product decisions are grounded in real-world evidence

Cross-Functional Leadership: Drive alignment across engineering, design, commercial, and client success teams. Drive sprint-level prioritisation within your squads and ensure the team is focused on the highest-impact work

Go-to-Market Execution: Partner with commercial and marketing teams to develop positioning, pricing, and launch strategies. Ensure users and internal teams are well- prepared for every release

Product Analytics: Define and monitor key product metrics. Use quantitative and qualitative data to inform decisions, measure outcomes, and continuously improve the user experience

Stakeholder Communication: Keep leadership and cross-functional stakeholders informed with clear, concise updates on progress, risks, and strategic decisions

What you’ll bring

5+ years of experience in product management, with demonstrated ownership of a product or product area end-to-end

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.

Proven ability to work effectively with engineering teams and translate technical complexity into clear product direction

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority across functions and seniority levels

Entrepreneurial mindset, you thrive in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and have a strong bias for action

Deep empathy for users and a passion for solving real-world problems in complex operating environments

Experience with Agile methodologies and tools such as Jira, Amplitude, or similar

Strongly regarded

Experience in the mining, geospatial, or survey industries

Track record of zero-to-one product development, taking a product from concept through to launch and market traction

Experience building or managing platform software products (APIs, SDKs, data pipelines, or developer-facing tools)

Experience with deep-tech or hardware-integrated products, where product decisions span physical and digital domains

The perks

9-day fortnight - every second Friday off

Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP) share in Emesent’s success

Work alongside a friendly and passionate team building cutting-edge technology

Genuine learning and development opportunities, including access to LinkedIn Learning

Employee Assistance Program

One full day of leave each year for your work anniversary

Novated Lease options

Who are we?

Emesent is a world leader in drone autonomy, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Founded in 2018 with deep roots in Australia’s globally recognised CSIRO, we’ve built a reputation for delivering high-quality spatial data capture across mining, infrastructure, survey, and construction industries. Our flagship product, Hovermap, is an autonomous mobile scanning platform that combines advanced collision avoidance and autonomous flight with LiDAR mapping to capture data in hazardous, GPS-denied, and hard-to-reach environments. Mountable on drones, vehicles, or carried as a backpack, Hovermap has set the standard for mobile mapping in underground mining and confined spaces. Our newest product, GX1, extends Emesent’s mapping technology into the AEC and survey markets. GX1 is a ground-based LiDAR scanner purpose-built for above-ground
environments delivering fast, accurate spatial capture for surveying and reality capture professionals.

With a rapidly growing global customer base and multiple product lines spanning hardware, desktop software, and cloud, Emesent is at an exciting inflection point  and we’re looking for people who want to help shape what comes next.


Why Emesent?
At Emesent, you’ll work on technology that is genuinely shaping the future of how industries capture and understand the physical world. We offer a dynamic, collaborative environment, competitive compensation, and real opportunities for professional growth. Join us in pushing the boundaries of robotics and spatial intelligence.

Classification:  Academic Level D

Terms: Full-time, Continuing

Position Description and Selection Criteria: PD & PEWER_Director,Australian Data Archive_Academic Level D.pdf

Position Overview

This is an exciting leadership opportunity as the Director of the Australian Data Archive (ADA), hosted by POLIS: The Centre for Social Policy Research at The Australian National University. In this role, you will lead the ADA as a national research data infrastructure service within POLIS, collaborating with partners across the University, Australian universities, government agencies and international standards organisations. The occupant of this position will be required to successfully manage the day-to-day operations of ADA at the local and national levels. The role includes responsibility for ADA data archiving processes, including archive and access activities, and coordination with the international organisations to support standards and tools which the Archive needs to maintain in its services. In addition, the occupant will coordinate ADA’s operational requirements in the development, design and implementation of ADA procedures for management of the ADA collection.

The Centre

The Australian Data Archive (ADA) provides a national service for the collection, preservation and dissemination of digital research data, enabling secure and sustainable reuse across social science and related domains.  The ADA is based in POLIS: The Centre for Social Policy Research which is designed to provide a designated space at the University for discussion, debate and research on the formulation of social policy. The Centre provides research and expertise on social policy in response to community and federal and state/territory government needs and requirements. POLIS delivers exceptionally robust data and evidence driven insights into the key challenges facing contemporary Australia. This provides the foundational cornerstones of informed social policy development amongst leading stakeholders within our modern policy landscape: government, community groups, business representatives, and educators.

The ANU College of Arts and Social Sciences (CASS) is one of seven Colleges at the University. The College, which is structured into two main research schools, offers degrees in more than 20 discipline areas and excels in research across the creative arts, humanities and social sciences. The College has a substantial international research presence and is a major source of national policy advice. Our academic staff are internationally recognised for their research, and 57 are members of the Australian Academy of the Humanities, the Academy of the Social Sciences of Australia, or both. We also host 13 Australian Research Council Future Fellows and three ARC Laureates. A hub of vibrant activity, we host more than 270 lectures, concerts and exhibitions each year, most of which are open to the public. Our students, staff and graduates come from more than 60 nations, bringing a diversity of perspective to campus life.

For enquiries regarding this role, please contact Professor Ben Edwards, E: Ben.Edwards@anu.edu.au

A highly prestigious financial services firm is seeking a Delivery Planning Lead with a couple of years of experience, to coordinate business changes within its treasury function.

With a background in financial services, you're comfortable with financial concepts & products and have strong skills around planning, prioritisation and stakeholder management.

With a reputation for being a fantastic place to work, truly valuing their employees and with an excellent culture, they are an excellent name to have on your CV!

Hit the Apply for this job button to find out more!

You are:

You're a business-focused Delivery Lead, ideally with a couple of years' experience in a similar position and a business background (a business-focused Business Analyst or change background would be ideal).  

Solid experience in financial services is essential; if you have worked in the treasury space that is perfect, but broader banking experience is fine, as you should have the foundations to quickly pick up capital models, interest models etc.

Excellent stakeholder engagement skills are imperative and you'll be hands-on, with strong planning skills.

The Role:

As the Delivery Planning Lead, you'll be overseeing many smaller business initiatives, working between their model team and model risk management team, defining & embedding the operating model.

The initiatives are less projects and more models that are built with requirements under the model standards that are then validated. These are a result of business changes that trigger updates and then models need to be validated with stakeholders across the group more broadly.

This role isn't right for a Delivery Lead who is used to managing large projects, or someone who's experience is managing tech initiatives. This will be more about reviewing changes coming in, assessing the deliverables and how they are tracking and engaging stakeholders across the wider business.

They are:

They are a hugely successful firm in financial services and are known to hire the best of the best.

With awesome CBD offices, they have a hybrid WFH/office model and flexible hours.

They have fantastic staff retention & are a great place to grow your career, with a big emphasis on promotion and limitless career advancement opportunities.

This team manages all changes for treasury and will give you exposure across all teams and financial products.
 
Responsibilities / Experience / Background:

Delivery Lead with background in financial services or insurance
A couple of years in a similar position, with a business background (business BA ideal)
Treasury experience would be amazing but not essential
Understanding of financial concepts & products
Resolve issues coming from the planning process
Oversee all changes for treasury
Focus on planning, prioritisation, stakeholder management
Embed operating models
Overseeing many smaller business initiatives
Treasury functions, model risk management & regulatory

About this role
As the Senior Exposure Management Analyst, you will own the operation of nib's Exposure Management function as a durable Line 1 capability.

The role is accountable for continuously identifying, prioritising, escalating, and tracking remediation of technology exposures, and validating that remediation is effective through retesting/verification. This includes unifying exposure signals across vulnerability management and posture sources into a consistent, risk‑aligned remediation view that supports operational risk reduction and regulatory obligations.

About you
We're looking for a security professional with strong experience in Exposure and Vulnerability Management, who can confidently drive end-to-end operations across identification, prioritisation, and remediation.

You bring a hands-on approach to managing vulnerabilities at scale-coordinating remediation efforts across teams, escalating where needed, and ensuring issues are effectively resolved through validation and retesting. Your ability to maintain momentum and oversight across the lifecycle will be key to strengthening our security posture.

You apply a risk-based mindset to everything you do, going beyond standard scoring models to assess exposure in context. Leveraging threat intelligence and real-world risk signals, you're able to prioritise what truly matters-focusing effort on the vulnerabilities that pose the greatest impact to the business.

You'll bring:

Relevant degree qualification in a related discipline (e.g., cybersecurity, information technology, computer science) and/or equivalent industry experience
Experience operating remediation tracking and reporting cadences with multiple technology teams, including planning cycles and stakeholder communication
Have / are working towards information security industry certification(s). Beneficial certifications may include ISC2 certifications, SANS/GIAC, and/or ISO/IEC 27001 certifications (preferred)
Working understanding of privacy and data protection obligations (e.g., GDPR awareness) (preferred)
Experience improving or enabling operational security reporting (e.g., tooling-driven reporting, posture metrics, remediation tracking or governance reporting enablement) (preferred)
Experience contributing to security capability maturity uplift activities such as technical workshops, campaigns, or continuous improvement initiatives (preferred)

Data Lead

We are seeking a dynamic and highly technical Data Lead to spearhead data engineering and analytics initiatives for an enterprise client. In this role, you will bridge the gap between complex enterprise systems and modern cloud architecture, turning raw data into actionable business intelligence.
You will lead, mentor, and scale a talented team of Data Engineers and Data Analysts. Operating within a fast-paced landscape, you will oversee the design, implementation, and optimisation of robust ETL/ELT pipelines, ensuring data streams are seamless, accurate, and highly scalable.

Team Leadership & Strategy
People Management: Lead, mentor, and foster the professional growth of a high-performing team of Data Engineers and Data Analysts.
Resource Allocation: Manage sprint planning, prioritise project backlogs, and align team deliverables with broader business objectives.
Data Governance: Establish and enforce best practices for data modeling, code quality, testing, and data security across the organisation.
Architecture & Data Engineering
Enterprise Integration: Architect and oversee ingestion pipelines from major enterprise systems (e.g. SAP ERP, CRM) into the central data ecosystem.
Cloud Infrastructure: Drive the evolution of cloud data, utilising Microsoft Azure or GCP services to build scalable, secure solutions.
Data Pipelines: Design, optimise, and maintain high-volume ETL/ELT workflows to support real-time and batch processing requirements.
Analytics & Business Enablement
Insights: Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver deep analytical insights 
Self-Service Analytics: Empower business units by designing clean, optimised data models (e.g., Star Schema) that fuel intuitive dashboards and reporting tools.
Technical Skills & Experience
Industry Focus: Ideally 10+ years of data experience, with a significant portion spent within fast paced environments (e.g. FMCG, Retail, Supply Chain/Logistics) 
Leadership: 3+ years of experience formally managing or leading a technical team of engineers and analysts.
Enterprise Systems: Strong hands-on familiarity working with SAP (e.g., SAP BW, S/4HANA) or equivalent enterprise-scale ERP data structures.
Technical Stack Proficiencies
Cloud Platform: Proven expertise in Microsoft Azure or GCP data components (e.g., Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Synapse, Azure Data Lake Gen2, GCP Pub/Sub, BigQuery, GCS, Dataform/dbt, Looker, Composer).
Data Processing: Advanced proficiency in SQL and Python for complex data manipulation.
Data Warehousing & Tools: Deep understanding of modern ETL/ELT frameworks, data orchestration (e.g. Airflow, dbt), and data lakehouse architectures (Delta Lake).
BI & Analytics: Familiarity with visualisation tools like Power BI or Tableau to guide the analytics team on delivery.
This is an awesome opportunity to work on a greenfield program with plenty of autonomy to really drive home what the data architecture and engineering roadmap looks like. If you're interested, please apply now!

Company Description


Empower Australia’s Payments Future with Cuscal  

At Cuscal, your skills drive change and make a real impact. Whether you’re supporting our clients’ customers in our Fraud Operations Contact Centre or delivering innovative solutions in Product Delivery, your contribution helps shape the payments industry’s future.  

Join a team that values collaboration, innovation and excellence. With a focus on professional growth and flexibility, Cuscal is where you can thrive.  

Forward with Cuscal.  

Job Description


As part of a conditional, binding agreement to acquire another Australian payment services provider, subject to regulatory approvals, we are looking for a Senior Project Analyst to join our Program team. 

As the Senior Project Analyst you will, provide oversight and support for the effective delivery of the project workstream’s outputs and products, ensuring that each project stream within the broader program is executed successfully.  

You’ll be responsible for driving the achievement of strategic objectives and benefits, maintaining delivery within budget, on schedule, and to the required standard of quality, in alignment with Cuscal’s Project Management and Project Investment Governance and Execution Frameworks.   

You’ll also make an impact by:  

Maintain integration governance with clear controls to manage progress, risks, issues, dependencies, assumptions and resources.  
Coordinate integration tasks with Program outcomes—synergy, culture, and operations—under direction from the Program Director and Integration Lead.  
Provide a consistent and precise overview of integration progress, enabling effective change management and informed decision-making regarding legacy systems, processes, and priorities.  
Apply best practice project governance frameworks appropriate to the Program and ensure data hygiene across program management systems e.g JIRA, Confluence.  
Collaborate with both internal and external teams to develop plans aligned with the agreed scope of work and manage stakeholders and their expectations.  
Manage executive reporting cadence to track integration milestones, synergy targets, and risk indicators using RAG status and clear stakeholder communication (as needed and applicable to the workstream).  
Ensure adherence to project governance frameworks, templates, and methodologies to drive quality delivery.  
Support Program budget management, including synergy tracking, transition costs, forecasting, contingency and resource utilization to ensure alignment with financial plans.  
Review business cases, funding proposals, and change requests for completeness and alignment with scope, schedule, and resource needs 

Qualifications


What you’ll bring  

You’ve got an inquisitive nature with the ability to see the bigger picture; you’ve got excellent communication skills and are detail orientated. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are passionate about Agile ways of working.   

To succeed in this role, you’ll have:  

5+ years of experience performing end to end project governance (PMO) of complex initiatives, covering business, technology and vendor engagement involving multiple industry stakeholders and multi delivery streams of work  
Experience of structured project methodologies, tools and recognised project management disciplines overseeing quality assurance and change processes and practises.  
Exceptional organisation skills and are skilled in influencing, guiding and facilitating stakeholders and peers with decision making  
Tertiary education/degree equivalent and have Prince2/PMP or equivalent  

Although not required the following experience would be highly desirable:  

Experience with mergers and acquisitions, or in fast-growing environment.   
Financial Services experience in payments and banking.  

Additional Information


Why Cuscal? 

At Cuscal, you’ll find a strong, successful company that’s reimagining the future. And our team is right there at the heart of it all.   

Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking work that has real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves.  

You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. 

And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow.   

You’ll also enjoy a range of benefits, including: 

Recognition and growth: Celebrate achievements through IGNITE and grow with tailored development opportunities. 
Wellbeing focus: Access initiatives supporting physical, mental, and financial health, plus discounts via ‘Cuscal Advantage.’ 
Diversity and inclusion: Join a workplace that values different perspectives and flexible work arrangements. 

Join us and reimagine the future 

Ready to make an impact? Click APPLY to join today. 

If you’re excited about this opportunity, we’d love to explore how you can contribute to our vision for the future. Screening and interviews may occur before the job ad closing date, so don’t wait - apply now. 

Cuscal is an equal opportunity employer committed to an equitable, diverse, and socially inclusive work environment and a positive, barrier-free recruitment process.  

We welcome applicants from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage, people living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and people from culturally diverse backgrounds to explore career opportunities with Cuscal.   

Note: Cuscal does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies or search firms.

Company Description


Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. 

Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. 

Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. 

We are majority-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. 

Please visit our website: www.turnerandtownsend.com 

Job Description


We are supporting a major defence programme in Adelaide, delivering a complex, long‑term portfolio of work of national significance. The programme requires strong governance, data‑driven decision‑making and clear performance insight to enable successful outcomes. This role sits at the heart of programme governance and reporting.

The Reporting Manager is responsible for delivering high‑quality, timely and consistent performance reporting and insights across a very large, complex programme of work. The role supports governance, strategic decision‑making and proactive performance management.

Key responsibilities include:

Develop, maintain and publish monthly dashboards and reports for all sub‑programmes within the reporting system.
Operate and continuously improve reporting systems to ensure data is accurate, timely and actionable.
Provide clear, insightful and tailored reporting to senior stakeholders, enabling evidence‑based decisions on performance and deliverability.
Work across cross functional teams, including with Reporting & Systems teams, embedding best practice in data visualisation, analysis and reporting.
Delivers to the reporting calendar whilst also fostering a forward-looking ethos at reporting trends, hotspots to enable preventative intervention
Ensure the reporting delivered is fact based, aligning stakeholders to one single source of truth
Have the ability to spot problems before they are problems and identify unreported risks and issues – filter through reporting noise to identify important signals

Qualifications


To be successful in this role, you will bring a blend of technical reporting expertise, leadership capability and stakeholder influence.

Essential qualifications and experience:

Degree (or equivalent) in Business, Finance, Engineering, Commercial, Project Management or a related discipline.
Proven experience delivering reporting and insights for large, complex programmes or portfolios.
Strong capability in performance measurement frameworks, reporting hierarchies and governance reporting.
Advanced data analysis and visualisation skills, with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights.
Demonstrated experience influencing and engaging senior stakeholders across multidisciplinary teams.

Desirable:

APM qualification (or equivalent) and experience across P3M environments.
Substantial experience with Power BI or equivalent reporting tools.
Experience within defence, utilities, water or other regulated industries.
Background in data quality management and continuous improvement initiatives.

Additional Information


Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change.

We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance. 

Turner & Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - AI Enablement (AU remote)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora,你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point. 

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney, with a second campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth, & Adelaide. You have flexibility in how and where you work — whether that's from one of our spaces, from home, or a mix of both. This role is remote-friendly within Australia, so you can choose the setup that empowers you and your team to do your best work.

Job Description


About the Group/Team

Canva's AI Platform Group serves as the foundation for AI innovation across the company, and achieving our GenAI ambitions depends on its ability to scale effectively. Within this group, the AI Enablement teams provide critical solution expertise to researchers and AI builders globally, helping them navigate platform capabilities, apply expert domain knowledge, optimise implementations, and accelerate the journey from experimentation to production. These teams bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and reliable, production-ready features, ensuring that innovation translates into real user value.

About the Role/Specialty

Please note, this is not a model building role

This role sits at the intersection of solution expertise and platform engineering, requiring deep technical expertise across ML domains combined with strong platform knowledge. The senior MLE will context-switch between facilitating model evaluations, data pipeline, optimising inference performance and working directly with product teams building solutions.

The nature of enablement work demands someone who operates as a force multiplier, finding satisfaction not just in solving technical problems but in enabling others to move faster. The role operates in a high-velocity environment where priorities shift rapidly based on team needs, requiring both technical excellence and pragmatic judgment. This combination of deep expertise and adaptive problem-solving is critical to accelerating the delivery of GenAI capabilities across Canva, removing friction from the path to production and ensuring product teams across the company can ship AI-powered features at the pace and quality our ambitions require.

What you’ll do (responsibilities):


Responsibilities will evolve as Canva's needs change, but here's what the role will focus on initially:

Act as a solution expert across ML domains including evaluations, training, inference, data pipelines, labelling, and optimisation

Work directly alongside product teams as a trusted partner, helping them navigate technical challenges and arrive at effective solutions

Provide expert guidance on platform capabilities, helping teams understand what's possible and how to get there efficiently

Develop blueprints, patterns, and paved roads that allow other teams to follow proven approaches and accelerate their own implementations

Debug and resolve complex issues, identifying root causes and sharing learnings broadly

Balance hands-on problem solving with strategic thinking about how to scale enablement impact across the organisation

You're probably a match if you have any of the following:

Strong Python expertise with experience in ML frameworks

Experience working with Kubernetes

Experience or knowledge of building data pipelines

Experience or knowledge of model evaluations

Experience with inference optimisation and serving infrastructure

Experience supporting or working closely with research, data science, or product teams

Excellent debugging and problem-solving skills across the full ML stack

Ability to context-switch effectively across multiple concurrent projects

Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to varied audiences

A collaborative mindset with a focus on enabling others to succeed

Additional Information


Don't tick all the boxes? Don't worry about that - nobody does!  

We’d still love to hear from you! At Canva, we know that great engineers come from a variety of backgrounds, and we value passion, curiosity, and a willingness to learn just as much as specific experience. If you're excited about this role but don’t tick every box, we encourage you to apply, you might a great fit in ways you didn’t expect!

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out  lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

All interviews are conducted virtually

Location: This role could be based out of any of our PwC offices in Australia




About us




At PwC, our people in risk and compliance focus on maintaining regulatory compliance and managing risks for clients, providing advice, and solutions. They help organisations navigate complex regulatory landscapes and enhance their internal controls to mitigate risks effectively.

In Actuarial services at PwC, you will be responsible for analysing and managing financial risks for clients through statistical modelling and data analysis. Your work will generate valuable insights and recommendations to help businesses make informed decisions and mitigate potential risks.




Why PwC?




At PwC Australia, your skills meet purpose. We tackle big challenges across industries like finance, technology, energy, and health—giving you the chance to make a real impact. Here, your growth is our priority. You’ll work with leading teams, explore new technologies, and unlock your full potential.




Join a global community of more than 370,000 people who value bold ideas, collaboration, and lasting change. Together, we’re building trust and shaping the future.




What you’ll do:

Analyse and identify the linkages and interactions between the component parts of an entire system.

Take ownership of projects, ensuring their successful planning, budgeting, execution, and completion.

Partner with team leadership to ensure collective ownership of quality, timelines, and deliverables.

Develop skills outside your comfort zone and encourage others to do the same.

Effectively mentor others.

Use the review of work as an opportunity to deepen the expertise of team members.

Address conflicts or issues, engaging in difficult conversations with clients, team members and other stakeholders, escalating where appropriate.

Uphold and reinforce professional and technical standards (e.g. refer to specific PwC tax and audit guidance), the Firm's code of conduct, and independence requirements.




What we’re looking for:

Practical exposure to prudential regulatory requirements and their implementation;

Excellent analytical and problem solving skills and enjoy working with clients to support on a range of projects;

Strong communication, project management and stakeholder management skills;

Working well in teams and can support and develop more junior team members in a high-performing and caring work environment; and

You hold a MSc / BSc (Hons) in Statistics / Actuarial Science / Financial Mathematics or Applied Mathematics as well as have programming capabilities in SAS / R / Python / SQL




What you could gain:

The opportunity to work with leading organisations and cutting-edge technology that challenge and expand your expertise.

Flexibility means trusting you to choose when, where, and how you work—balancing what’s best for you, your team, and your clients to grow and thrive together.

Clear paths for career progression supported by continuous learning and leadership development.

Generous leave entitlements including four weeks’ annual leave and float public holidays to support your work-life balance.
